back_translation: All the work that Solomon did for the house of the LORD was completed. Then Solomon brought in the things that David his father had dedicated — the silver, the gold, and all the vessels — and put them in the treasuries of the house of God.

Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel, all the heads of the tribes, and the leaders of the families of the Israelites, in Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the covenant of the LORD from the City of David, which is Zion.
So all the men of Israel gathered before the king at the feast in the seventh month.
When all the elders of Israel had arrived, the Levites took up the ark.
They brought up the ark, the Tent of Meeting, and all the sacred vessels that were in the tent; the priests and the Levites carried them up.
King Solomon and the whole congregation of Israel who had assembled before him offered sheep and oxen as sacrifices before the ark, so many that they could not be counted.
Then the priests brought the ark of the covenant of the LORD to its place, into the innermost room of the house, the Most Holy Place, beneath the wings of the cherubim.
For the cherubim spread out their wings over the place of the ark, and the cherubim covered the ark and its carrying poles from above.
The poles were so long that the ends of the poles could be seen projecting from the ark in front of the inner room, but they could not be seen from outside; and they are there to this day.
There was nothing in the ark except the two stone tablets that Moses had placed there at Horeb, where the LORD made a covenant with the Israelites when they came out of the land of Egypt.
When the priests came out of the Holy Place — for all the priests who were present had consecrated themselves, without regard to their divisions —

and all the Levitical singers — Asaph, Heman, Jeduthun, with their sons and brothers — dressed in fine linen, stood on the east side of the altar, holding cymbals, harps, and lyres, and with them were one hundred twenty priests sounding trumpets;

when the trumpeters and singers joined together as one to make one sound in praise and thanksgiving to the LORD, and when they lifted up their voice with trumpets, cymbals, and instruments, praising the LORD, “For He is good; His steadfast love endures forever,” then the house, the house of the LORD, was filled with a cloud,
so that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud, for the glory of the LORD filled the house of God.
